notes: IDology PASS -> PASSED.
Flagged pass collapses to PASS.

Unknown bright_uid -> 404 INVALID_USER. [FIXED] Was pointed at /start with an all-zeros uid; /start get_or_creates the user (HandleInitiate) so it (a) can't 404 and (b) had already provisioned the all-zeros uid, which then returned NO_VALID_FLOW_FOUND. Repointed to /resume with a fresh random uid that is never provisioned -> user-existence check fails first (views.py:278-281) -> INVALID_USER.

Invalid user-data param -> 400 INVALID_USER_DATA. [FIXED] Was a bare /start (expect 400), but /start takes no data params and can never emit INVALID_USER_DATA; that code is /resume-only (views.py:290-292) and runs before the flow-status block (race-independent). Now: /start 200 creates the User+KycFlow, then /resume carries additional_data_params.invalid_field (not a ParamKey) -> 400 INVALID_USER_DATA.

PO-box address code -> FAIL_ADDRESS. [UPDATED] Idology fail routes through LN(any) -> Persona create PENDING -> resume persona_kyc_verified=PASS -> park PENDING at ESCALATE_ADDRESS. [FIXED] LexisNexis NAS RiskCode 2->7 (NAS_NON_SSN_FAIL_CODES, per real lexisnexis/constants.py): NAS=2 is an SSN-fail code, so trigger 30 ({8:FAIL_SSN,9:PASS}->ESCALATE_SSN) tied in specificity with the intended field-specific trigger and won the id tie-break, masking it. NAS=7 removes trigger 30 from contention so the intended ESCALATE_* fires uncontested.
